posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

It’s amazing how this image of a spider keeps returning throughout weaving myths and across countries that would have had no contact with each other.
In Navajo and Hopi tradition it’s described through two seperate beings, Mama Ocllo and Spider Grandmother.
*Typo on slide 5, it should say ‘This ISN’T ancient folklore…’ sorry 😅
Swipe to learn more and listen to the full episode on The Culture of Cloth wherever you get your podcasts 🎧

posts.caption:
This was a huge milestone for me because it taught me something I needed to learn.
Working in the fashion industry taught me that what I physically produced and created was equivalent to my worth.
Images and visuals are the primary language in the fashion industry, especially when communicating with manufacturing teams whose first language might not be english.
So I didn’t put much value on my writing or my philosophical views around fashion and culture. It was always a dialogue that was happening inside me, something I didn’t think connected to my sewing work.
But this year I’ve really leant into writing and finding my voice on Substack.
I think I can get hung up on thinking I need to sound a certain way to be taken seriously, so it’s been a learning curve to find what my voice actually sounds like.
I’m still learning and figuring it out but I’m really enjoying the dynamic of Substack and the space to write out my thoughts when they don’t fit into an Instagram carousel.
So here are my articles since I started in March.
Read the full articles via the link in my bio 🔗
First slide image is my Cordelia sewing pattern with my Hera sleeve sewing pattern mixed together 🥰 shop via the link in my bio

posts.caption:
Rembrandt once gave us instructions on how to view his work….”Don’t stand too close.” he said.
This week was session 3 of @readingsbooks Big Book Club on The Odyssey and we learned a little history about oral poetry.
Dr Tom Hercules Davies, a comparative philologist at @unimelb actually sung a few lines from the poem to help us understand the important of the musicality of the text.
Genuinely, it was so fascinating!
Read the full essay via the link on my bio
In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.alt_text:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.

She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”

Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.

For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile. 

Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: Size inclusivity in menswear remains largely static across #MilanFashionWeek and #ParisFashionWeek, with this season also welcoming more polarized body ideals. Alongside the continued dominance of straight-size casting, there was a sharp focus on extreme physiques, from "protein chic" and hyper-muscular builds shaped by looksmaxxing, to the return of ultra-slim "slender man" silhouettes. We found that of the thousands of looks presented across Milan and Paris SS27 shows, only 0.2% were shown on plus-size models, remaining unchanged since FW26. Tap the link in bio for a break down of the full report.

posts.caption:
Soon, @FrancescoRisso’s designs at @GU_Global will be seen, shopped, and styled by the masses. With his first collection on the way, Risso tells Vogue Business he is aware he doesn’t fit the formula perfected by Fast Retailing, the Japanese retail empire that owns Uniqlo, Theory, Helmut Lang, and GU. "I’m a bit weird in the recipe," he says. Having spent eight years designing at Prada before joining Marni in 2016, Risso is looking forward to delivering something more accessible at GU — as luxury fashion prices continue to rise.
At the link in bio, the creative director sits down with executive editor @HilaryMilnes to shed light on how fashion creatives are approaching their new jobs at mass retailers.

posts.caption: The latex look is having a moment. On the runways, both latex and latex-looking materials have dominated this summer — ironic, given it’s on track to be one of the hottest. It’s making a mark in pop culture, too. At @YSL’s SS27 show in Paris, mid-heatwave, #ConnorStorrie rocked up in knee-high latex sock boots. Once relegated to subcultures, will this trend translate to ready-to-wear — and sales? @MaddieeSchulz speaks to those in the industry to get their thoughts.

For their first Vogue India cover together, Padmanabh Singh (@pachojaipur) and Gauravi Kumari (@gauravikumari) turn a day in Singapore into a sibling sport. Born one year apart, they grew up like twins with very different operating systems. Gauravi is “the planner, the pseudo-mom”, while Padmanabh continues to baffle her with spontaneous travel plans and a living-out-of-a-suitcase lifestyle.
Read the July-August cover story at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Purti Pareek (@pareeek.p) and Amit Persaud’s (@amitpersaud) wedding in Barwara became a multigenerational homecoming with personalised rituals and conscious design choices. One of the most meaningful departures from convention was replacing kanyadaan with kanyamaan, reworking the ritual towards mutual respect. They also chose not to have a vidaai. “I was not going anywhere. I wanted to maintain the joyous atmosphere of combining our lives,” says Pareek. Throughout their wedding, the couple’s guiding brief was simple: To have as much fun as possible.
Words by Palak Godara (@_palakgodara)
Photographed by The Blemished Tales (@theblemishedtales)
#VogueWeddings

posts.caption: Paparazzi. She always looks good, but it would be nice if photographers considered celebs to a certain extent. I realize those tabloid mags sell because I bought thousands of them, but obviously they have family and friends too. Also they might promote those magazines more and have them in their homes or offices for a good reason, not a bad one. That would actually increase sales. Making someone look bad is just not nice. She happens to be dressed well, but this could have made her look a little better. I like USWeekly and People magazine the most.

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.

posts.caption:
@Juju Smith-Schuster and @Laura_Kruk have tied the knot. The wedding took place at the Ritz-Carlton in Laguna Niguel, California, on July 10, 2026. The celebratory weekend began with a Hawaiian beachside welcome party in Laguna Niguel, a nod to the groom’s Samoan roots. The next day, the ceremony was set on a lawn overlooking the ocean adorned with white and green florals. The guest list was star-studded, even including Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce. “Seeing all of our family and friends mingling and making new friends was so fun to see.” Tap the link in our bio for more on the coastal California celebration.
Photos: @nicoleivanovphoto

Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.

She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”

Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.

For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile. 

Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

Good things come to those who wait. For the final night of Jay-Z’s three sold-out shows at Yankee Stadium, @badgalriri made her long-awaited return to the stage, joining the rapper in custom @ysl, no less. And she wasn’t the only surprise of the night: @beyonce, @teyanataylor, @pharrell and many more also took the stage, making for a memorable evening. At the link in bio, see how Rihanna revisited her “Music of the Sun” era with her fashions.

posts.caption: The weather may have been hotter than hell, but Paris’s fall 2026 couture shows delivered heavenly head-to-toe details.Hints of glittering gold at @chanelofficial and @dior, smoky wings at @GiorgioArmani Privé, the eternal wet look at Michael Stewart’s @Standing_Ground_, and chalk-dusted ballet hair at @JulieDeLibran took center stage on the runway. The couture week beauty brief spanned Gilded Age glam to zero-part hair, and beyond. Discover the 4 standout trends from Vogue Beauty’s couture report at the link in bio. Backstage photographed by @styledumonde
posts.alt_text:
The most important step in @SiennaSpiro's beauty routine? Ensuring every product she uses is cruelty free. For the latest installment of Vogue's #BeautySecrets, the singer shares her skin-care and beauty holy grails, and reveals her signature eyeliner technique. Tap the link in our bio to watch the full video. 

Director: @gabriellereich
DP: @ebmfletch
Editor: @sara.rao
Associate Producer: @leadonenberg
Filmed at: @ludlowhotelnyc

Size inclusivity in menswear remains largely static across #MilanFashionWeek and #ParisFashionWeek, with this season also welcoming more polarized body ideals. Alongside the continued dominance of straight-size casting, there was a sharp focus on extreme physiques, from "protein chic" and hyper-muscular builds shaped by looksmaxxing, to the return of ultra-slim "slender man" silhouettes. We found that of the thousands of looks presented across Milan and Paris SS27 shows, only 0.2% were shown on plus-size models, remaining unchanged since FW26. Tap the link in bio for a break down of the full report.

posts.caption: “Taormina.. seems made to seduce the eyes, the mind and the imagination,” once wrote Guy de Maupassant of a spot in Sicily that has been renowned for its breathtaking beauty since 300 BC. Tonight @DolceGabbana presented its Alta Moda show in Taormina for the second time: the first was back in 2012 when they first launched this family-style and molto Italiano alternative to haute couture. Jennifer Lopez, Monica Belluci and Christian Bale joined around 300 clients to see a collection heaped with flowers, trademark house lace and corsetry, and classical references galore. The show was held in @radicepura - a sprawling botanical garden used as a location in The Godfather Part II. “For us Alta Moda is about love,” said Domenico Dolce as the after-party warmed up.

Backstage photographed by @styledumonde

posts.caption: Haute couture is often referred to as fashion’s laboratory. By collaborating with scientists, Iris van Herpen has turned that metaphor into reality: Her fall 2026 collection included a dress made, in part, by immaterial plasma. While the Dutch designer was focused on things intergalactic, Christian Dior’s Jonathan Anderson had been studying the work of the American multimedia artist Lynda Benglis. Elsewhere, Standing Ground’s Michael Stewart, ArdAzAei’s Bahareh Ardakani, and Rahul Mishra explored folklore related to their home countries, and fairytales tickled the imaginations of Chanel’s Matthieu Blazy and Robert Wun—though this was not exactly a season of child’s play. At the link in bio, we count down the 13 trends of the fall 2026 haute couture season.

posts.caption: Artists Jacky Marshall (@jackyblue__) and Blue Farrier (@bluefarrier) close out the fall 2026 couture season with drawings from @viktorandrolf and @jeanpaulgaultier. The dynamic duo of Viktor Horsting and Rolf Snoeren explored the contrast between restraint and decadence while Duran Lantink made his couture debut at Gaultier with clothes for a Marie Antoinette down the rabbit hole kind of scenario. Tap the link in bio to see the exclusive collection drawings.

posts.caption:
On Thursday evening, inside a white-brick warehouse in Paris’s Belleville neighborhood, Martin Margiela’s archive went under the gavel in a one-of-a-kind auction marking the first time a living designer has directly collaborated with an auction house to sell their personal collection.
Over five hours, ‘Martin Margiela: Personal Archives’ saw almost 200 lots go under the hammer, including select pieces from his Hermès years, sourced from the wardrobe of his late mother, Léa Boucher, and unique pieces he had created during the pandemic. Most bidders in attendance were fashion archivists, fans, or museums, with the auction raising over €1.3 million, a portion of which will go to AIDS charities.
At the link in bio, contributor @SLWilson goes inside the auction, speaking with organizers about how it came together and attendees about what they got their hands on.
Auction Photos: Niña Slavcheva

posts.caption:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.
With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces.
For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.

Floral design: @temperature_official

Jewellery: @tiffanyandco
Talent: @chua.tanya

posts.alt_text:
Curiousity never killed the cat. So at 51, @chua.tanya is still doing hardcore gymnastics; an indisputable reflection of what has always been a lifelong resolve—to always try the things that scare you. 

"I’m doing backflips and handstands at an age where most people would be terrified to try. But if you’re constantly fearful of failure, nothing is going to get done. You’ll never do anything interesting or out of the ordinary," our 'Audacity' cover star proclaims. 

In the link in bio, the Singaporean singer-songwriter's narrative may read mercurial yet there was always a steadfast nerve behind it all: be it back when she debuted with her English album in 1997, when she wanted to do a tour dedicated to her less recognised tracks or even when she decided to take up gymnastics later in life. 

The July/Aug 'Audacity' issue is available for pre-order, and will hit newsstands on 22 July.

posts.caption:
The streets of Seoul; a cacophony of sounds; a smorgasbord of sights. Earlier this year, the South Korean capital played out as the vibrant locale in which @chanelofficial hosted its replica show for its Métiers d'art 2026—one which @shiiiannn sat front row at. The emerging starlet and #VogueSingapore's digital cover star for July/August has been on the up and up, with roles spanning 'Amoeba', 'We Learn to Breathe in Distant Places' and 'Secondary: The Musical'.
With performances marked by a rare duality—earnest yet incisive, tender yet unflinching—she brings a striking depth to every character she inhabits. Similarly, for her time in Seoul, she brings her introspective nature to various locations that feel intrinsically part of the city's cultural fabric, as #VogueSingapore lenses her in the chaos of a pocha, or in the company of Buchaechum dancers.
In the link in bio and our July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, the Singaporean darling shares more about her craft, dream projects and ties to the fashion world.

Photographer: @nikolaiahn

posts.caption:
Tucked away in a fortress deep in Montenegro is @BanyanTreeHotels’ newest—and first ever—sanctuary in Europe, on @MamulaIsland.
Overlooking the cerulean Adriatic Sea, what was once an Austro-Hungarian fortress in the 19th century has been transformed into a secluded getaway that welcomes slow island living while paying homage to the grandeur of its medieval past.
Whilst its secluded location offers one unrivalled privacy, there is no shortage of activities in the itinerary: think open water adventures, Banyan’s signature artist-in-residence programme, and visits to local Moric family farms to discover how Montenegro’s finest olive oil is made. In the link in bio, follow lifestyle writer @phatnoise as she checks into @mamulaisland, and explores all the resort has to offer. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
Following a monumental 14-year tenure at the helm of Balmain, @Olivier_Rousteing has been formally appointed to @Rabanne as the new creative director. The French designer, known for his distinct public persona and transformation of Balmain’s commercial trajectory, will succeed Julien Dossena.
“Olivier Rousteing’s creative vision is bold, making it deeply connected to the energy of today,” said Ana Trias, president of prestige and fashion brands at @Puig, in a statement. “Olivier Rousteing’s creative vision is bold, magnetic, and deeply connected to the energy of today. His unique ability to create fashion that celebrates confidence and self-expression makes him a natural choice for Rabanne. With Olivier, we are opening a new chapter for the house.”
Rousteing’s first collection for the Spanish luxury maison will arrive in March 2027. #VogueSingapore
📷: @Rabanne

posts.caption: Beauty, flair and haute horlogerie. These are the meeting points of the RM 07-01 collection, the quintessential feminine line by the Swiss brand @richardmille. Whilst slender and fabulous on its own—the playful Coloured Ceramics models has offered a new dimension to the line since 2021. And this year? A final chapter is unfolding, with three chic and cheerful models—introducing the technical feat of gem-setting into ceramics, a play that the brand has long mastered. Discover more about the triptych of RM 07-01 Coloured Ceramics timepieces, now in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.

posts.caption:
: A strong marriage isn't built on grand gestures alone—it's built on consistent connection.
Sometimes, the biggest intimacy mistakes aren't intentional. From assuming your partner knows how you feel to letting busy schedules replace quality time, these small habits can quietly create distance over time.
Healthy intimacy grows through communication, appreciation, trust, emotional presence, and making your partner feel loved every single day. ❤️

posts.alt_text:
KAVITAON KA SILSILA AA RAHA HAI @sohotheatre LONDON. 💌🫀

They say everything iconic has once happened at SOHO (@sohotheatre ). The thought that Rajkumari will now be counted among the stories told on that stage still doesn’t feel real.

It’s strange… I can often find words for emotions people have buried deep within them, but when it comes to this show~my own, I always fall short.

Rajkumari has changed my life in ways I don’t think I’ll ever fully be able to put into poetry.

The last time we were in London, we promised we’d return bigger and better.

We kept that promise.

This is our biggest London show yet, and I’d love for you to be in the room with us, to laugh, cry, heal, and become a part of this chapter.

Tickets are in my bio. See you at Soho. 🔗🤍

@tarashna.management @beacon.live

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra @rahulmishra_7 presented ‘Devi’ at Haute Couture Week, Paris. Drawing inspiration from ancient South Indian sculptures and the divine feminine, the collection was an absolute showstopper.
The lineup effortlessly transformed featherlight fabrics into breathtaking, structured silhouettes. By blending traditional artistry like intricate hand embroidery, zardozi, and dabka with modern design; Mishra beautifully celebrated India's rich artisanal heritage. The collection's contemporary edge was further elevated through brilliant collaborations with clay artisan Sumant Kumar, legendary milliner Stephen Jones, and Tanishq Natural Diamonds, seamlessly breathing new life into centuries-old craftsmanship.
Jewellery by @tanishqjewellery

posts.caption:
"Listen. I’m not going to be mad at anyone calling me beautiful,” @lupitanyongo says. In Director Christopher Nolan's much-anticipated, much-shrouded blockbuster adaptation of The Odyssey, Nyong'o plays Helen of Troy, the mythic face that "launched a thousand ships." When asked how it sits with her to be playing one of the most beautiful women of all time, Nyong'o responds enthusiastically, "Really well!" While many women struggle to accept a single compliment, the Oscar-winning actress is fully owning her turn as the legendary Greek figure. After all, regal, striking beauty is inextricable from Nyong'o's public identity: as Nakia, the warrior/spy and love of the late Chadwick Boseman's King T'Challa in Black Panther; as the first Black face of Lancôme; and as a Chanel ambassador. But what fans may not realize is that Nyong'o is pulling double duty in the film by portraying Helen's sister Clytemnestra too.
In our July cover story, @michandrews speaks to Nyong'o about starring in the cultural event of the summer, letting her work speak for itself, and how her ongoing battle with uterine fibroids sparked the Make Fibroids Count research campaign. Read the full interview at the link in our bio.
Photographer / Creative Conception: @knngrm
Stylist: @jan.quammie
Creative Direction: @bonatalia
Hair Stylist: @vernonfrancois
Hair Stylist Assistant: @batiseolhair
Makeup Artist: @nickbarose using @chanel.beauty
Makeup Assistant: @golfxmua
Manicurist: Bagir Hasanov
Set Designer: @lane.vineyard
Editor in Chief: @katcollings
Entertainment Director: @jbake21
Writer: @michandrews
posts.alt_text:
"I remember seeing Whoopi Goldberg in more than one movie, and I was like, 'Wait, you could do this?'" @lupitanyongo tells @michandrews. Growing up, Nyong’o never dreamed of acting as a viable career path. "I come from a very different world,” she emphasizes. Nyong’o is the daughter of a Kenyan politician/professor and a public health advocate, and was raised between Kenya and Mexico City. It’s moving now for her to imagine her career providing the same kind of recognition for a new generation. "I feel very honored to be anybody's Whoopi,” she adds.

Read more in our July cover story at the link in our bio.

Growing up, Nyong’o never dreamed of acting as a viable career path. "I come from a very different world,” she emphasizes. Nyong’o is the daughter of a Kenyan politician/professor and a public health advocate, and was raised between Kenya and Mexico City. It’s moving now for her to imagine her career providing the same kind of recognition for a new generation. "I feel very honored to be anybody's Whoopi,” she adds.
Read more in our July cover story at the link in our bio.

posts.alt_text:
"Listen. I’m not going to be mad at anyone calling me beautiful,” @lupitanyongo says. In Director Christopher Nolan's much-anticipated, much-shrouded blockbuster adaptation of The Odyssey, Nyong'o plays Helen of Troy, the mythic face that "launched a thousand ships." When asked how it sits with her to be playing one of the most beautiful women of all time, Nyong'o responds enthusiastically, "Really well!" While many women struggle to accept a single compliment, the Oscar-winning actress is fully owning her turn as the legendary Greek figure. After all, regal, striking beauty is inextricable from Nyong'o's public identity: as Nakia, the warrior/spy and love of the late Chadwick Boseman's King T'Challa in Black Panther; as the first Black face of Lancôme; and as a Chanel ambassador. But what fans may not realize is that Nyong'o is pulling double duty in the film by portraying Helen's sister Clytemnestra too.

In our July cover story, @michandrews speaks to Nyong'o about starring in the cultural event of the summer, letting her work speak for itself, and how her ongoing battle with uterine fibroids sparked the Make Fibroids Count research campaign. Read the full interview at the link in our bio.

The best wardrobes aren’t built overnight. They’re collected, discovered, and shaped by the stories each piece carries.
There’s something about pre-loved pieces that makes the search feel different. Less about shopping, more about discovering something that was always meant to find you.

posts.caption:
A mix of usual and unlikely beauty suspects won Amazon’s most recent Prime Day sale, which came early this year and ran from June 23 through the 26th rather than in July.
Data from Navigo Marketing shows that @nutrafol was the top beauty brand during the sale, comprising an estimated 3.6 percent of beauty sales, mainly for its $88 Women’s Balance Hair Growth Supplement.
@medicube_global_official, which in the last year has been encroaching on Nutrafol’s top shareholder rank, came in at number two, making up about 3 percent of sales.
Read the full details on the top performers and why they won on WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @noorlobaddie
posts.alt_text:
Since it’s taking place in the U.S., the 2026 version of @fifaworldcup final, scheduled for Sunday at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J., is to get a Super Bowl-inspired halftime show.⁠
⁠
But one constant will be that Louis Vuitton will provide the trophy trunk, as it has since 2010, further underscoring the French luxury brand’s motto that, “Victory travels in Louis Vuitton.”⁠
⁠
The bespoke 2026 version will be dressed in the brand’s iconic gold and brown monogram canvas and feature a hand-painted gold V.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Miles Socha

posts.caption:
@Olivier_Rousteing, who spent most of his career at Balmain, is @Rabanne’s new creative director. He is expected to hold his first show for the Puig-owned house in March.
He first became aware of Paco Rabanne when he was about 12 years old and joined his grandparents in their living room in Bordeaux, France, to watch a TV documentary about the Spanish fashion legend, which flashed images of Françoise Hardy, Brigitte Bardot and Audrey Hepburn wearing his shiny, Space Age designs.
“He’s always been part of my mood board,” he said. “Paco Rabanne is a legend as a man, as someone known for his craft, and as a couturier who really embraced pop culture.”
Rousteing officially started at Rabanne on July 6, and is to present a pre-fall collection in November, with the first runway show during Paris Fashion Week in March 2027.
Read Editor International @milessocha’s exclusive interview with the designer at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Photos: Francesca Beltran/Rabanne, Fairchild Archive/WWD

posts.caption:
“The Odyssey” is just days away from arriving in theaters, and its press tour has already created plenty of fashion headlines. Grecian-inspired styling has been the unofficial dress code for the stars promoting the film, including Charlize Theron, who has worn Dior, Givenchy and Tom Ford, among other designers, throughout the tour.⁠
⁠
But beyond the mythological bent of Theron’s “Odyssey” red carpet and photo call wardrobe, what if there was more to the styling? She may have been channeling her character, the sea nymph Calypso, all along.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Julia Teti⁠
Photos: Getty Images for Universal Pictures, Zak Hussein

posts.caption:
This crush looks likely to be here to stay.
For those crushing on @chanelofficial, after Coco Crush Fine Jewelry, there’s now a new fragrance: Coco Mademoiselle Crush Absolu. It launches 25 years after the original floral-ambery Coco Mademoiselle entered the market.
“Coco Mademoiselle truly holds a very special place in la maison Chanel and in the heart of our clients, because this fragrance is not just a scent,” explained Simona Cattaneo, president of Chanel Fragrance and Beauty. “It embodies something much bigger. It’s really the spirit of the young Gabrielle Chanel arriving in Paris. She was instinctive. She was bold, with a sensuality all her own. So it was kind of — wow.”
Read European beauty editor @jenniferbethweil's full interview with Cattaneo and Chanel perfumer creator Olivier Polge on the launch at WWD.com — #linkinbio
📸: François Goizé/WWD

posts.caption:
@theofficialpandora has launched Pandora Wonders, a multi-year platform showcasing Pandora’s commitment to playful design and artisanal craftsmanship.
This first chapter, co-created with British stylist and creative Harry Lambert  focuses on freshwater pearls, reinterpreted through the lens of contemporary culture.
The limited-edition charms featuring an ice cream cone, a space rocket, a mouse, a subterranean squid and a mushroom are crafted using one-of-a-kind baroque pearls. Every pearl differs, with each selected and collected for its specific shape on the charm. Handset by Pandora’s artisans, the pieces from the collection charms are detailed with 14-karat gold-plating.
Read more at #linkinbio
